People take advantage of each other: by looking down on the poor, they criticize their Creator. This behavior is shameful for anyone, but who can strongly dislike it enough when adopted by those who claim to be Christians? With compassion for the oppressed, we should grieve over the difficulties that many in the world are suffering; putting ourselves in their place, and remembering in our prayers and assistance those who are burdened. But let those who show no kindness expect judgment without kindness.

Nehemiah knew that even if he built Jerusalem's walls as high, thick, or strong as possible, the city could not be safe while there were abuses. The right way to change people’s lives is to convince their consciences. If you live in reverence for God, you will neither be greedy for worldly possessions nor cruel toward your brothers and sisters. Nothing exposes religion more than the worldliness and lack of compassion among those who profess it. Those who insist strongly on their rights try to persuade others to give up theirs with very little grace. When reasoning with selfish people, it is helpful to contrast their behavior with that of others who are generous; but it is best to point to His example, who though he was rich, became poor for our sakes, so that through his poverty we might be rich. They acted according to promise. Good promises are good things, but good actions are better.

Those who truly fear God will not dare to do anything cruel or unjust. Let all those in positions of authority remember that they are there to do good, not to enrich themselves. Nehemiah mentioned it to God in prayer, not as if he deserved any favor from God, but to show that he depended only on God to compensate him for what he had lost and spent for His honor. Nehemiah spoke and acted like someone who knew himself to be a sinner. He did not mean to claim a reward as something owed to him, but in the way the Lord rewards a cup of cold water given to a disciple for his sake. The fear and love of God in the heart, and true love of brothers and sisters, will lead to every good work. These are proper signs of saving faith; and our reconciled God will look favorably on people of this character according to all they have done for His people.
